advantages: Usually the shortest, simplest algorithm — often just sort + sweep.
Fast: typically O(n log n) due to the sort, O(n) per scan.
Easy to extend with a priority queue for streaming use cases.
When provably correct, no need for memoization tables or backtracking.
disadvantages: Wrong for many problems where the greedy-choice property fails (0/1 knapsack, general coin change).
Silent failures: returns *a* answer that looks plausible but isn't optimal.
Correctness usually requires a proof — easy to convince yourself wrongly.
Heuristic greedies (e.g. greedy decoding) trade quality for speed and need eval to confirm.
